---
title: "GitHub Cheat Sheet"
subtitle: "Things I do infrequently enough to not remember how to do them, but frequently enough to need to know"
---

### Orphan branch

To create an orphan (empty) branch with no commit history in an existing repository:

1. Create a new branch

   `{bash} git checkout --orphan newbranch`

2. Delete the existing content

   `{bash} git rm -rf .`

3. Commit the changes

   `{bash} git commit --allow-empty -m "New empty branch"`

4. Push the changes, if linked to a remote repository

   `{bash} git push origin newbranch`

Use this when creating a dedicated `gh-pages` branch for a GitHub Pages site or a `docs` branch for documentation whose commit history is separate from the code itself.
